properties.title: Fuel stop — Wegmans?
properties.thought: The Navigator is very soft. Probably too soft for whats ahead. Ten kilometers from launch ... and he is already stopping for food.
properties.status: all systems nominal.
properties.photoCaption: One last stop before saying goodbye to Wegmans until the fall.

properties.title: Camp — East Harbor
properties.thought: Too much pavement. Too many cars. Need to find some gravel on the way north. Soon.
properties.status: above 32C all day. too hot. where is the Arctic?

properties.title: Buttercup Wild Camp-day 2 and 3
properties.thought: vibe is good. day 2 and i've already found -- with a bit of help from The Navigator -- sweet double track and an empty wild camp.
properties.status: system nominal. Navigator in a tizzy. something about ticks everywhere. i wonder about him.
properties.photoCaption: sweet Michigan double track on way into camp

properties.title: Grace makes her migration 2026 debut
properties.status: too nice of a day for rest.
properties.photoCaption: Grace excited to get some mud on her tires, then enjoy a relaxing float.

properties.title: Tranquility found
properties.thought: Evenings like this make it easy to love Canada
properties.status: refigrator on the fritz. will monitor.
properties.photoCaption: Lake Superior from Pancake Bay.

properties.title: Steelhead sighting
properties.thought: Steelhead fishing means gravel roads or, even better, double track. So I am a fan.
properties.status: refigrator still on the fritz.
properties.photoCaption: An Agawa River steelhead, caught and released by The Navigator.

properties.status: refigrator still on the fritz.

properties.title: Coaster sighting
properties.thought: And brook trout fishing also leads to large amounts of gravel consumption. So I am still a fan.
properties.status: refigrator still on the fritz.
properties.photoCaption: An Agawa River lake-run brook trout, aka coaster, caught and released by The Navigator.

properties.title: Another coaster sighting
properties.status: refigrator thinking about working again.

properties.title: wild camp on the Steel River
properties.status: refigrator is working. reason unknown.
properties.photoCaption: A perfect evening. Cool. Some sun. No bugs.

properties.title: another wild camp on the Steel River

properties.title: kakabeka falls camp

properties.title: sow with coys sighting

properties.photoCaption: Sow with cubs sighting on day 12.

properties.title: Wild camp at edge of Agassiz National Wildlife Refuge
properties.thought: Nearly 75 miles of gravel and dirt roads today.
properties.status: Wild camp established for the night just outside of the refuge.

properties.title: Trumpeter swan sighting in Agassiz National Wildlife Refuge
properties.status: Departed from the Agassiz area and continued west on day 13.

properties.title: Camp near Surrey, ND
properties.thought: Rode the tailwind for nearly the entire day!
properties.status: Camp set at the end of day 13 for the night.

properties.title: Camp at Dog Creek on BLM land
properties.thought: The weather was challenging today, with rain, wind, and cold temperatures.
properties.status: Rainy. Windy. Cold. But camp set at the end of day 14 route.

properties.title: Camp at Bozeman Hot Springs, MT
properties.thought: Overhead The Navigator talking to Overland Cruisers about a suspension upgrade. Starting to feel like Christmas.
properties.status: Camp set for the next three nights.

properties.title: Suspension upgrade at Overland Cruisers, Belgrade, MT
properties.thought: Dobinson MRR shocks and coils installed. The ride feels tighter, more planted, and ready for industrial-level gravel consumption.
properties.status: Suspension upgraded.
properties.photoCaption: New Dobinson MRR shocks and coils installed at Overland Cruisers.

properties.title: Camp in Missoula, MT
properties.status: Camp set in Missoula, MT for a few days.

properties.title: Taking a break at the old Milton Dam site during the Dear Creek Sneak bike ride with Andrew
Blackfoot River sighting
properties.photoCaption: Floated the Blackfoot River with Andrew. High water!

properties.title: Salmon fly hatch sighting
properties.photoCaption: Salmon flies were flitting around all day. Won't be long before the blizzard arrives.
properties.photoNote:
Salmon flies (Pteronarcys californica) are among the largest stoneflies in North America — adults can reach nearly 2 inches. They hatch once a year, usually triggered by water temps climbing into the mid-40s °F, and the hatch window on a given stretch of river is only a few days.

When it happens, it's chaos. The bugs come off in clouds so thick you can smell them. Trout gorge themselves to the point of being almost unhookable — they barely need to move to eat.

Waldo spotted this cluster clinging to streamside willows near the takeout at the old Johnsrud bridge. Andrew reckoned the main event was still a day or two out upriver. The water was running high and fast — maybe 6,000 cfs — which pushes the fish tight to the banks and into the slower eddies.

This is why people plan trips to the Blackfoot in June.

properties.title: Camp at rest stop along Rt 212 in South Dakota

properties.title: Slept at rest stop at Illinois/Indiana border
Stormy Night at Dolly Sods
properties.thought: The Navigator is calling this Day 1 of Attempt 2. I'm still counting it as Day 25.
properties.photoCaption: Habitat module deployed at Red Creek Campground in Dolly Sods. Windy, overcast skies and dense forest surround the site.
properties.photoNote:
Crossing the country without melting in a heatwave or burning in a wildfire. An even bet, at best.

A more southern route this time. Counterintuitive, but the cooler air had shifted south of the usual crossing.

A cold front rolled in as I rolled out of Maryland. Rain most of the way to Dolly Sods — I did not object.

The Navigator recalibrated the Stauns: 3.5 turns for 30 psi. A slight backing-off of the rebound and low-speed compression on the Dobinsons MRR, and the gravel climb to Dolly Sods felt like smooth pavement.

A stormy night at 4,000 feet in West Virginia. I'm exactly where I'm supposed to be.
